title: Nine Out Of Ten
description: A mad scientist conducted $n$ independent identical experiments and claimed that $x$ of them were successful. It is well known that the mad scientist is wrong in exactly 90\% of cases when determining the success of a single experiment. Your task is to write a program that calculates the minimum and the maximum possible number of successful experiments for all $x$ from $0$ to $n$. It is guaranteed that the total number of experiments is always divisible by $10$.
input: The first line contains a single integer $n$, which is a multiple of ten ($10 \le n \le 10\,000$).
output: Print $n + 1$ lines. On the $i$-th line, output two integers separated by a space: the minimum and the maximum possible number of successful experiments for $x = i - 1$.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Northern Eurasia, Northwestern Russia Regional Contest, ICPC 2025-2026 Northwestern Russia Qualification
